sections.paragraph:
περισσοτέρως adv. of περισσότερος (s. two prec. entries; Diod S 13, 108; Athen. 5, 19f; PGiss 25, 12 [II a.d.]).

① (even) more comp. Mk 15:14 v.l.; to a much greater degree, far more, far greater (than Paul’s opponents) 2 Cor 11:23; (than those of his own age) Gal 1:14. (Opp. ἧσσον) 2 Cor 12:15 (ἀγαπάω 1aα). Intensifying so much (the) more Phil 1:14; Hb 2:1; 13:19.

② especially, elative, 2 Cor 1:12; 2:4; 7:15; (all) the more 1 Th 2:17. Strengthened περισσοτέρως μᾶλλον even much more 2 Cor 7:13.—DELG s.v. περί. M-M.

adv. adv. = adverb, adverbially

prec. prec. = preceding

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Athen Athen , III a.d.—List 5

PGiss PGiss = Griechische Papyri . . . zu Giessen—List 4

comp. comp. = comparative(ly)

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 806
